name: Harris Financial Coaching
description_short: Individual financial coach offering budget planning, credit management, and retirement guidance to families. AFCPE-affiliated with 16 years experience and financial coaching certification.
description_long:
Harris Financial Coaching is a sole proprietorship founded in 2021 by Annette Harris, based in Jacksonville, Florida. The business operates as an independent coaching practice focused on personal financial empowerment rather than as a traditional non-profit credit counseling agency.

The service offers personalized financial coaching through phone and video consultations, priced at $459 per fixed-price session. Core offerings include budget development, credit understanding and management, retirement planning, debt management, credit building, wealth-building strategies, and facilitation of family financial conversations. Harris brings credentials including a Financial Fitness Coach certification from Sage Financial Solutions (2022), SPHR certification, a Juris Master in Legal Compliance, and a Masters in Human Resources Management.

Harris distinguishes herself through a dual focus on immediate financial coaching for adults and long-term financial literacy education for young people. The coach specifically targets parents seeking to teach children budgeting skills before they leave home, aiming to instill wealth-building mindset in the next generation. She holds AFCPE affiliation (2021) and offers specialized expertise in retirement planning, military benefits evaluation, and employment benefit assessment.

Key limitations exist: Harris operates as a solo practitioner rather than a non-profit organization, charges consultation fees ($459 per session), and appears to lack the HUD-approved housing counselor credentials or NFCC certification typical of traditional free-help credit counseling agencies. While AFCPE-affiliated, the practice is fee-based rather than subsidized. The service is best suited for individuals seeking personalized coaching who can afford professional fees, rather than those requiring low-cost or no-cost credit counseling.
address: 11111 San Jose Blvd Ste 56 #1333, Jacksonville, FL 32223

company_info.headquarters: 11111 San Jose Blvd Ste 56 #1333, Jacksonville, FL 32223

pros: AFCPE-affiliated financial coach with legitimate credentials (Financial Fitness Coach certification from Sage Financial Solutions, 2022)
Dual expertise in HR management and financial coaching, bringing employment benefits evaluation and career transition guidance
Specialized focus on teaching children financial literacy and budgeting before adulthood
Bilingual coaching capability (English fluent, Spanish intermediate)
16 years of professional experience across HR and financial coaching domains
Addresses difficult family conversations about money, a gap many traditional counselors don't emphasize
Covers retirement planning, military benefits, and employment benefit evaluation alongside basic credit guidance
cons: Fee-based service ($459 per session) rather than free or subsidized, contradicting 'free-help' category placement
Solo practitioner with limited capacity compared to established non-profit credit counseling networks
No HUD-approved housing counselor credentials or NFCC certification mentioned, which are standard in free-help category
Limited public information on client success rates or testimonials available on profile
Coaching modality (1-on-1) may not scale to serve high-volume consumers compared to agency-based counseling
best_for: Parents and households seeking personalized financial coaching who can afford $459 session fees
Families wanting to instill wealth-building mindset and budgeting skills in children
Individuals transitioning careers who need combined HR guidance and financial planning
People with military background seeking specialized benefits evaluation alongside general financial coaching
diagnosis: Harris Financial Coaching is best for affluent or middle-income families seeking personalized financial coaching and who prioritize teaching children financial literacy. The primary caveat is that despite 'free-help' categorization, this is a fee-based service ($459/session) without non-profit credentials, making it inappropriate for consumers seeking free or subsidized financial counseling through traditional NFCC or HUD-approved channels.
services: Personal financial coaching via phone and video consultation
Career guidance and employment transition coaching
Financial literacy education for young people and teens
